reasoning: Total EU imports of Russian natural gas (pipeline and LNG) have been steadily declining. Data for 2025 shows a significant further drop due to the expiration of the gas transit agreement through Ukraine in January 2025. Specifically, Bruegel data for Q2 2024 showed 13 bcm; preliminary reports for Q2 2025 indicate a 'two-fold contraction' (to roughly 6.5 bcm). LNG imports from Russia for the first 8 months of 2025 aggregated to 14 bcm, averaging 5.25 bcm per quarter. TurkStream, the other major pipeline route, has a total capacity to the EU of only about 15.75 bcm per year (~4 bcm per quarter). Combined, these sources (LNG + TurkStream) are projected to yield around 9-10 bcm per quarter throughout 2025, which is significantly below the 15 bcm threshold. Given the geopolitical environment and infrastructure constraints, there is no realistic pathway for a quarterly spike to 15 bcm.
